behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: discovery of `.sql` files from `migrations/`, numeric-prefix ordering, and execution order against the DB. Hypothetical elsewhere: cross-process races, OS-level file event quirks, network faults. MOCK-MISS: failure is at module boundaries (file discovery -> sort -> execution), not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/subprocess requirement beyond local filesystem+DB, no AI output, no agent chain. Rejection-gate: System was considered, but behavior text does not require separate processes or external network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp `migrations/` directory with out-of-order filenames (e.g., `002_b.sql`, `001_a.sql`, `010_c.sql`) and SQL that appends markers into a table. Run the runner against a local test Postgres DB and assert markers show execution in numeric-prefix ascending order.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: `_migrations` state persistence and rerun skip behavior across runs. Hypothetical elsewhere: multi-process contention, OS/subprocess failures. MOCK-MISS: bug appears when runner state and metadata table interact over multiple invocations. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no required real subprocess/network condition, no AI dependency, no agent pipeline. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior only specifies subsequent run semantics, not concurrent processes or OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the runner twice against the same local test Postgres DB with a migration that has a visible side effect (e.g., insert one row). Assert side effect count remains 1 after second run and `_migrations` contains one record for that migration.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: transactional rollback semantics and stop-on-failure sequencing in one run. Hypothetical elsewhere: cross-process locking races, host OS scheduling artifacts. MOCK-MISS: in-memory fakes can hide real transaction behavior; needs real local DB transaction handling across modules. REAL-INFRA: requires DB behavior, but this fits Integration (local DB) per level defs; no required subprocess orchestration. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: System considered for stronger realism, but behavior text does not require separate process/OS-level interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Use local test Postgres with three migrations: first valid, second intentionally failing mid-transaction, third valid. After run, assert first migration effects persist, second migration effects are absent (rolled back), third migration was not applied, and `_migrations` records only successful prior migrations.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: true concurrent execution from separate processes against same DB and exactly-once global effect. Hypothetical elsewhere: AI quality, multi-agent orchestration. REAL-INFRA: explicit separate-process timing and DB locking behavior; in-process mocks can pass while real process interleavings fail. MOCK-HIDE: yes, mocked concurrency would hide race conditions. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn two real runner processes simultaneously against the same Postgres instance and identical `migrations/` directory. Wait for both to finish; assert each migration’s side effects occur exactly once total and `_migrations` has one row per migration with no duplicates.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the `_migrations` table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: initialization ordering between metadata-table bootstrap and first migration application on a fresh DB. Hypothetical elsewhere: cross-process races, OS/subprocess specifics. MOCK-MISS: failure is in startup wiring/order across modules (bootstrap -> apply), not isolated pure logic. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: local DB+filesystem suffices; no AI dependency or multi-agent sequence. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior does not require separate process behavior, only correct initialization order in one run.
behaviors.test_description: Start from an empty local test Postgres schema and a migration set whose first migration expects `_migrations` infrastructure to exist. Run once and assert `_migrations` table exists and bootstrap completed before migration effects are applied.
